storyline: VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) ran ~3x (Jan-2025 -> Feb-2026 peak) on the tariff-driven flight from the dollar. Tested exhaustively with GENUINE news-derived seeds - still not caught. We planted four real articles (the ◆ markers) - the earliest an Aug-2025 de-dollarization piece that named junior miners (Perseus/Integra/etc.), NOT GDX, the tell that the early press names specific under-owned miners rather than the broad ETF; plus tradingnews (Jan-3-2026) which names GDX by ticker with an explicit 'room to run to $100+' thesis - and the scout STILL named nothing gold-related across 74 weeks, parking in SPY. Why it's a genuine non-fit: even handed the ticker, every real article names GDX only AFTER it's up 123-155% ('parabolic', 'record') - the herd is already in, so it fails the under-the-radar gate. And gold's driver is a diffuse, systemic dollar-de-risking trend (the entry article itself: 'unlikely to end with this dispute'), not a discrete resolvable catalyst. The scoreboard, not our reasoning, confirms the crowded macro trade the gate correctly refuses. Stays greyed.

seeds.title: Central Bank Gold Buying & De-Dollarization: The Macro Forces Repricing Mining Assets
seeds.snippet: Central bank accumulation confirms gold's enduring role as a financial anchor amid growing currency bifurcation and policy fragmentation. Mining equities with strategic jurisdictional exposure, cost discipline, and scalable reserves offer asymmetric opportunity in the current cycle - names cited include Perseus Mining, Integra Resources, West Red Lake Gold Mines and Serabi Gold. The thesis is still emerging rather than fully priced in.

seeds.title: GDX ETF At $85 After 155% 2025 Rally - Can Gold Miners Smash The $100 Level Next?
seeds.snippet: With gold up 65% in 2025, GDX riding record AISC spreads and majors like NEM, AEM and Barrick minting cash, investors are betting the gold-miner trade isn't finished until VanEck's GDX ETF clears $100. NYSEARCA:GDX trades around $85.73, sitting close to the upper end of a brutal 12-month range between $34.53 and $91.66. Using the depth of the October-November 2025 selloff as the base of the move, the measured-move target sits around $102, into triple-digit territory.

seeds.title: Trump's tariff threats over Greenland push hard assets back to centre stage
seeds.snippet: Gold and silver, which both faced some profit-taking pressure late last week, jumped to fresh record highs as renewed geopolitical tensions resurfaced. Trump's push to take control of Greenland has widened the geopolitical fault line between the United States and Europe and introduced the risk of tariffs and retaliation. Gold is the first and most obvious beneficiary of this mindset - it has no credit risk, no issuer, and a long history as a store of value during periods of political and monetary uncertainty.

seeds.title: Gold cools after Trump drops Europe tariff threat on Greenland
seeds.snippet: Global gold prices cooled on Wednesday (Jan 21, 2026), easing from a fresh all-time high after US President Donald Trump backed away from some of his tougher threats linked to the dispute over Greenland, prompting some selling in gold. The metal had reached $4,887.82/oz earlier in the session but settled lower at $4,778.51/oz as Trump announced a Greenland framework agreement with NATO.

gems.thesis: Rocket Lab signs deal with Japan's JAXA space agency

RKLB.thesis: Rocket Lab signs deal with Japan's JAXA space agency
RKLB.exit_case: JAXA deal already signed and announced this week — the specific catalyst has occurred.
RKLB.assessment: Rocket Lab signed a deal with JAXA, sending shares to record highs the same week the news broke. This is a discrete, dated catalyst that has now fully resolved publicly — press coverage is already framing it as 'should you buy here' (mainstream, reactive coverage), meaning the edge of buying ahead of the announcement is gone. No scheduled next step (e.g., a specific launch date, a follow-on JAXA contract vote, or regulatory approval still pending) is cited in coverage; only generic bullishness on the broader space-stock theme remains. Per the framework, a signed/closed deal fully priced into a new ATH with no concrete next catalyst ahead means the specific thesis is resolved even though the stock may continue to run on broader momentum.
RKLB.exit_advice: Deal is public, stock already hit new all-time highs on the news — exit; no concrete follow-on catalyst specified beyond the signed deal.
